name: home-it
description: Home IT skills. Ships check-network: a repeatable playbook to diagnose a slow home network end-to-end (measure throughput / bufferbloat / packet loss / Wi-Fi signal from the affected device, localize the bottleneck across ISP plan → router → Wi-Fi path → client, inspect the router admin UI) and rank fixes — wired backhaul first, a second wireless extender never. The same SKILL.md files back the Claude Code plugin.

interface.shortDescription: Diagnose and speed up a slow home network
interface.longDescription: Home IT skills for the learn-yy-skills marketplace. check-network is a repeatable playbook for a slow home network: measure from the affected device (throughput, idle-vs-loaded latency/bufferbloat, packet loss to the gateway, Wi-Fi signal and negotiated rate), localize the bottleneck across four layers — ISP plan, router, Wi-Fi path, client — inspect the router admin UI (QoS, band steering, channel, mesh/EasyMesh backhaul), then recommend fixes ranked by leverage: wired backhaul first, relocation and channel tuning next, a new router only if needed, and a second wireless extender never. The same SKILL.md files back the Claude Code plugin.
defaultPrompt: My home Wi-Fi is slow at my desk — can we make it faster?
Should I buy an extender or a new router?
